---
title: "Gerardo Dirzo Uribe"
toc: false
---

::: {.profile-header}
::: {.profile-left}
![](../images/equipo/gerardo.jpg){.profile-photo-large}

::: {.profile-links}
[✉](mailto:gerardo.dirzo@posgrado.ecologia.edu.mx)
:::
:::

::: {.profile-right}
**PhD Student**  
Instituto de Ecología, A.C. (INECOL)

*Interests:* [Bats]{.profile-tag} [Mammals]{.profile-tag} [Macroecology]{.profile-tag}

I am a biologist from FCB-UAEM, where I modeled the niche and distribution of the Volcano Rabbit (*Romerolagus diazi*) at different spatial scales. I obtained my Master's degree at CIByC-UAEM, where I assessed niche conservatism in the genus *Rhogeessa* (Chiroptera). My PhD research at INECOL focuses on assessing the relationship between Grinnellian and Eltonian niche breadth and patterns of richness and geographic distribution of phyllostomid bats.

### Education

- **PhD** — Instituto de Ecología, A.C. (ongoing)
- **MSc** — CIByC-UAEM (2021)
:::
:::

::: {.profile-back}
[← Back to Team](../equipo.html)
:::
